title: Drip vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Campaign Monitor email marketing tools. See pricing, features, ratings, and which is best for your business.
h1: Drip vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Drip and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences with distinct strengths. Drip is purpose-built for e-commerce businesses seeking deep store integration and revenue tracking, while Campaign Monitor excels at helping agencies and teams manage multiple brand identities. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize e-commerce automation or multi-brand management capabilities.
choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need revenue attribution and store integration
You want SMS marketing capabilities built into your email platform
You need advanced automation with visual workflow builders designed for online sellers
choose_b_if: You manage multiple client accounts or brand identities
Price is your primary concern and you need to minimize monthly costs
You prioritize beautiful email templates and design flexibility over e-commerce features

faq.q: Is Drip better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Drip has higher G2 ratings (4.4 vs 4.1) and is purpose-built for e-commerce with superior revenue tracking and SMS capabilities. However, Campaign Monitor excels at multi-brand management and costs significantly less, making it better for agencies. Choose Drip for e-commerce, Campaign Monitor for agencies.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor is substantially cheaper, starting at $11/month compared to Drip's $39/month. This makes Campaign Monitor the clear winner for budget-conscious businesses or agencies managing tight margins. However, Drip's higher price reflects its specialized e-commerce features like revenue attribution and SMS.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms support list imports and exports, allowing you to migrate your contacts between tools. However, you'll need to rebuild your automation workflows and campaigns in Drip since they don't transfer automatically. Plan for transition time and test thoroughly before switching your active campaigns.
